---
title: "Week 8 Milestone Analyze"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') 
# |>
#   select(ID, BDAT, FDAT, LACT, ARDAT, Date, Event, Remark, R, T, B, Protocols,
#          source_file_path)
  
# load GC's functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# pull solution data
#source('../functions/fxn_milestone7_solution.R')


# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)


```

```{r}

# lame data test new function
# 
all_lesions <- c("dd", "footrot", 'wld', "sole_ulcer","injury", 
                             "cork", "other", "hemorrhage",
                             "sole_fracture", "toe_ulcer", "thin",
                             "inf", "noninf", "toe", "axial", "lesion")

diseases <- c("inf", "noninf", "lesion")

lame_data <- events_formatted |> 
  select(id_animal, date_birth, date_fresh,lact_number, date_archived, date_event,
         event, 
         remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, location_event) |> 
  filter(event %in% c("LAME", "TRIM")) |>
  fxn_code_lesions(protocol_var = protocols_letters1,
                  remark_var = remark_letters1) |> 
  # fix ab and NA issues
  filter(!is.na(trimonly)) |>
  mutate(noninf = case_when(
    other == 1 & str_detect(protocols_letters1, "Ab") ~ 1,
    .default = noninf),
    # remove other catergory if abscess is found
    other = case_when(
      other == 1 & str_detect(protocols_letters1, "Ab") ~ 0,
      .default = other)
    ) |> 
  fxn_collapse_lesions(lesions = all_lesions) |> 
  fxn_trim_vars(location_event, id_animal,
                date_var = date_event,
                trimonly_var = trimonly) |> 
  fxn_dz_status(disease_cols = diseases,
                event_filter = trimonly == 0)


slice_lame <- lame_data %>% slice_sample(n = 1000)

start_time <- Sys.time()
test_dt <- lame_data |> 
  lazy_dt() |> 
  group_by(location_event, id_animal) %>% 
  # calculate times trimmed in data set
  mutate(times_trimmed = dense_rank(date_event)) %>% 
  arrange(times_trimmed) %>%
  mutate(         # determine if trim (1) or lame trim
         type_prev_trim = lag(trimonly, n=1),
         date_prev_event = ymd(lag(date_event, n=1)),
         # time dif for any trim type
         days_from_prev_event = date_event-date_prev_event,
                  # calc for lame cows time from last trim if last was a trim
         days_from_trim = if_else(trimonly == 0 & type_prev_trim == 1,
                                date_event - date_prev_event, NA),
         days_to_next_event = lead(days_from_prev_event, n=1),

         days_trim_to_next_lame = lead(days_from_trim, n=1)) %>% 
  ungroup() |> 
  as_tibble()
end_time <- Sys.time()
diff_time <- end_time - start_time

# try this to replace above for speeed

# 1. Arrange once at the start (much faster)
start_time_opt <- Sys.time()
dt_optimized <- lame_data %>%
  lazy_dt() %>%
  arrange(location_event, id_animal, date_event) %>% 
  group_by(location_event, id_animal) %>%
  mutate(
    # Use row_number if date_event is unique per animal
    # it is as slice min in fucntion made it so
    times_trimmed = row_number(), 
    
    # Pre-calculate lags to avoid repeating function calls
    type_prev_trim = lag(trimonly),
    date_prev_event = lag(date_event),

    
    # Vectorized date subtraction
    days_from_prev_event = date_event - date_prev_event,
    
    # logic for days_from_trim
    days_from_trim = if_else(trimonly == 0 & type_prev_trim == 1,
                             days_from_prev_event, NA),
    
    # Leads
    days_to_next_event = lead(days_from_prev_event),
    days_trim_to_next_lame = lead(days_from_trim)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  as_tibble() # Or as.data.table() for even more speed
end_time_opt <- Sys.time()
diff_time_opt <- end_time_opt - start_time_opt

test_dt <- test_dt %>% arrange(location_event, id_animal, date_event)
dt_optimized <- dt_optimized %>% arrange(location_event, id_animal, date_event)

waldo::compare(test_dt, dt_optimized)

diseases <- c("inf", "noninf", "lesion")

# test count fxn.

test <- lame_data |> 
  fxn_dz_status(disease_cols = diseases,
                event_filter = trimonly == 0)


# 





```


```{r}
#| label: functions to hide my code
#| include: false

lame_dim_graph <- function() {
  solution_dim_cat <- solution_data |> 
  mutate(dim_event = as.integer(difftime(date_event, 
                                         date_fresh, units = "days"))) |> 
  mutate(dim_cat = case_when(
      dim_event < 30 ~ "<30",
      dim_event <= 90 ~ "30-90",
      dim_event <= 150 ~ "91-150",
      dim_event <= 210 ~ "151-210",
      dim_event <= 270 ~ "211-270",
      dim_event > 270 ~ ">270",
      .default = NA_character_
    ),
    dim_cat = fct_relevel(
      dim_cat,
      c(
        "<30", "30-90",
        "91-150", "151-210",
        "211-270", ">270"
      )
    )) 


lame_data <- solution_dim_cat |> 
  filter(event %in% c("LAME", "TRIM")) |>
  fxn_code_lesions(protocol_var = protocols_letters1,
                  remark_var = remark_letters1)




lame_plot <- lame_data |> 
  filter(date_event >= max(date_event) - months(12)) |>
  filter(lact_number>0) |> 
  group_by(location_event, dim_cat) |>
  summarize(across(all_of(all_lesions), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |> 
  ungroup () |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(all_lesions), 
               names_to = "lesion", 
               values_to = "count") |> 
  filter(count >0) |>
  mutate(lesion = fct_reorder(lesion, count)) |>
  mutate(lesion = fct_recode(lesion, !!!lesion_labels)) |>
  ggplot()+
  geom_col(aes(y = lesion, x = count, fill = fct_rev(dim_cat)),
           color = "white")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(title = "DIM Category",
       x = "Count of Lesions in Lame Events",
       fill = "",
      y = "")+
  scale_fill_viridis_d()+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1, reverse = TRUE))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(
        hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold", size = 12
      ),
    legend.position = "top",
      legend.box = "vertical",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}


```

```{=html}
<!-- The objective of this milestone is to use the template report you created last week to create another report while learning how to call functions from other places.
Then you will learn the skills from this week to create a graph that takes data from text and date fields. You will use factor skills to order the graph properly to make your point cleaer.
A few notes:  1) I put the code on how to insert an image in the header in the yaml change it to use your logo. 2)Any data (including farm name) that is reported in the text should use an inline text code chunk -->
```


# Report for `r farm_name`

-   This report shows data from the past year from **`r date_min_pull`
    to `r date_max_pull`**.

## Objective

Your objective is to recreate the solution data by doing the following with the raw_data table.

-   id_animal is a combination of source_file_path, ID, BDAT
-   Herd is extracted from the source_file_path
extract the protocol data from the text fields
-   protocols1 and remark1 contain the first set of character strings before a non character string is found.
-   convert the exported data fields to real dates

## Your Code Creation

```{r}
#| label: your code

# put your code here to create the parsed variables.
```


```{r}
#| label: create-solution-data

solution_data <- events_formatted_all |> 
         select(id_animal, date_birth, date_fresh,lact_number, date_archived, date_event,
                event, 
         remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, R, T, B, location_event)  



waldo::compare(solution_data, solution)



```
### Create DIM Categories

Then on the new data table.  

-  Create a dim_cat that splits DIM into 6 categories.
-  Order this variable in the correct order so if you were to graph it the categories would make sense and would not be in numerical or alphabetical order

```{r}
#| label: dim-cat-order
#| echo: true

# put your DIM category code here



```

### Import Function

Then import this function from Github
This function will create new variables for LAME and TRIM remarks in your data set that now has clean protocol and remark fields and an associated dim_cat variable.
The function is called fxn_code_lesions() and it takes 3 input variables
event_var, protocol_var, remark_var.  

```{r}
#| label: import-functions
#| echo: true

# 1. Install/Load required library
if (!require("httr")) install.packages("httr")
library(httr)

# 2. Define the repo details
user <- "Dairy-Cow-Foot-Doc"
repo <- "os_functions"

# 3. Get the list of files in the repo via GitHub API
req <- GET(paste0("https://api.github.com/repos/", user, "/", repo, "/contents/"))
file_list <- content(req)

# 4. Filter for .R files and source them
for (file in file_list) {
  if (grepl("\\.R$", file$name)) {
    # Get the 'raw' version of the file so R can read the code
    raw_url <- gsub("github.com", "raw.githubusercontent.com", file$html_url)
    raw_url <- gsub("/blob/", "/", raw_url)
    
    message("Sourcing: ", file$name)
    source(raw_url)
  }
}

```

### Create New Variables

Then use this function to create new variables for the lesions in your data set.
```{r}
#| label: add-new-variables
#| echo: true

# put your code for recoding the variables here

```


## Plot Data
-  Summarize this new data for the past 12 months to create a graph that looks similar to this 

```{r}
#| label: fig-example_lame
#| fig-cap: "Lameness Lesions by DIM Category"
#| echo: true

# this might be helpful for your grap labels.

all_lesions <- c("dd", "footrot", 'wld', "sole_ulcer","injury", 
                             "cork", "other", "hemorrhage",
                             "sole_fracture", "toe_ulcer", "thin",
                             "inf", "noninf", "toe", "axial", "lesion")


# you can use this with the !!! we discussed last week to relabels the factor
  lesion_labels <- c(
    "Digital Dermatitis" = "dd",
    "Footrot" = "footrot",
    "White Line Disease" = "wld",
     "Sole Ulcer" = "sole_ulcer",
    "Hemorrhage" = "hemorrhage",
    "Sole Fracture" = "sole_fracture",
     "Toe Ulcer" = "toe_ulcer",
     "Thin Soles" = "thin",
     "Corkscrew Claw" = "cork",
     "Injury" = "injury",
     "Infectious" = "inf",
     "Non-Infectious" = "noninf",
     "Toe" = "toe",
     "Axial" = "axial",
     "Other Lesions" = "other",
     "Lesion Present" = "lesion"
  )


  
plot <- lame_dim_graph()

plot




```


    
# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would want to report

Ideas (or hints for next week include)

start doing math with dates so we can calculate time to event data (cull/repeat)
